 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","title":"PlasmaFx: Port Core Wave Function (Cell-Space)","description":"## Task
Port the core plasma wave function to cell-space rendering with explicit sampling steps.

## Scope
- Base wave equation (sum of sines) with normalized coordinates.
- Quality-controlled sampling step and palette lookup.
- Separate math from color mapping.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: wave output bounds and repeatability.
- Determinism test: fixed seed/time yields identical output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wave math stable for all buffer sizes.
- No NaNs or out-of-range color values.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:13.593473623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:57.606503958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:57.606446439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:13.593473623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":181,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Source reference: `PlasmaState::render()` in `visual_effects.rs`.
- Full mode currently uses ~6 trig evaluations per point.
- Reduced/Minimal modes should cut trig calls (e.g., 3 waves) and/or use cheaper approximations.

Acceptance:
- [ ] No per-frame allocations.
- [ ] Deterministic output given inputs.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","title":"PlasmaFx: Theme-Derived Palettes + Presets","description":"## Task
Provide theme-derived palettes and plasma presets tuned for legibility.

## Scope
- Palette generation from `ResolvedTheme` with fallback defaults.
- Presets: Subtle, Neon, Monochrome.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: palette stop counts and bounds.
- Snapshot tests: presets produce distinct visuals with readable overlay text.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Palettes are deterministic and theme-aware.
- Default preset is safe for all themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:16.451163090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:41:58.234835733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Palette strategy:
- Use 2-4 accent colors from ThemeInputs.
- Provide interpolation function (lerp) and multi-stop sampling.
- Keep it local to visual-fx; do not depend on text-effects APIs.

Presets:
- AuroraPlasma (cool)
- EmberPlasma (warm)
- NeonPlasma (high saturation)

Acceptance:
- [ ] Palettes look good across themes.
- [ ] Palette switching does not require allocation.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","title":"PlasmaFx: Determinism + Performance/Quality Tests","description":"## Task
Add determinism and performance/quality tests for PlasmaFx.

## Tests
- Determinism: fixed seed/time yields identical output.
- Quality: lower quality reduces sample count and render time.
- Snapshot: golden frames for preset + quality combinations.
- Allocation: no per-frame allocations after warm-up.

## Logging
- JSONL logs include seed, quality, palette ID, and timings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover determinism, performance, and quality behavior.
- Snapshot diffs are stable across platforms.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:35.260426176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:26.606820101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:35.260426176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Tests:
- spatial variance: sample multiple points and ensure not all identical
- determinism: same inputs -> same output hash
- quality honoring: Full vs Reduced produces different output but same dimensions

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests are robust and do not depend on demo code
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:40Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.5","title":"Demo Integration (Phased): Reuse Buffers + Adopt Extracted FX","description":"Refactor ftui-demo-showcase to (1) eliminate per-frame allocations in VisualEffectsScreen, (2) adopt extracted ftui-extras backdrops for metaballs/plasma, and (3) optionally keep hi-res rendering via canvas adapters — split into explicit subtasks for parallelism.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Demo Integration (Phased): Reuse Buffers + Adopt Extracted FX
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs
- If any palette/gradient math: specify the color space + constraints (e.g., OKLCH) and prove WCAG targets in tests
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:10:46.369515349Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Current Problem

`VisualEffectsScreen::view()` currently does:
- `let mut painter = Painter::for_area(...)` each frame

This allocates (pixels + colors vectors) every frame and undermines "insanely high performance".

## Refactor Goals

- Metaballs and Plasma should call the extracted `ftui-extras` code (no duplicated algorithms).
- Avoid per-frame Painter allocation by storing painter(s) inside screen state and resizing only on terminal resize.
- Keep existing transitions/controls.

## Implementation Notes

Approach A (preferred):
- Extract effect *core* so it can render both:
  - cell-space (BackdropFx)
  - painter-space (Canvas adapter)
- Demo uses painter-space to keep the wow factor.

Approach B (acceptable interim):
- Use Backdrop-only rendering for metaballs/plasma in the demo (lower-res but still good), while other effects keep using painter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No per-frame Painter allocations in steady state.
- [ ] Metaballs and Plasma demo visuals are driven by library code.
- [ ] No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:14Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Plan Update: Split Into Phases (Unblock Perf Work)

This issue was previously blocked on `bd-l8x9.3`/`.4` (extracted effects) and `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters).

That made the demo perf fix unnecessarily wait on library extraction.

New structure:
- Phase 1: stop per-frame allocations in `VisualEffectsScreen` (Painter reuse + resize-only growth)
- Phase 2: switch metaballs/plasma to the extracted `ftui-extras` BackdropFx implementation using **cell-space** rendering (lowest coupling)
- Phase 3 (optional): switch metaballs/plasma back to **hi-res** via canvas adapters (keeping the wow factor) once adapters exist

Each phase is tracked as its own subtask with the correct blocking dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:08Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-l8x9.5","title":"Demo Integration (Phased): Reuse Buffers + Adopt Extracted FX","description":"Refactor `ftui-demo-showcase` to (1) eliminate per-frame allocations in VisualEffectsScreen, (2) adopt extracted `ftui-extras` FX for metaballs/plasma, and (3) optionally keep hi-res rendering via canvas adapters.

This bead is intentionally phased to unblock perf work early:
- `bd-l8x9.5.1`: Painter/Canvas reuse (no per-frame allocations)
- `bd-l8x9.5.2`: metaballs/plasma via extracted BackdropFx (cell-space)
- `bd-l8x9.5.3` (optional): regain hi-res via canvas adapters
- `bd-l8x9.5.4`: fix CLI help text numbering so humans + scripts agree

## Acceptance Criteria
- VisualEffectsScreen is allocation-free in steady state.
- Metaballs/plasma paths in the demo are driven by `ftui-extras` (no duplicated algorithms).
- No behavior regressions in input handling or transitions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:50.704445082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Current Problem

`VisualEffectsScreen::view()` currently does:
- `let mut painter = Painter::for_area(...)` each frame

This allocates (pixels + colors vectors) every frame and undermines "insanely high performance".

## Refactor Goals

- Metaballs and Plasma should call the extracted `ftui-extras` code (no duplicated algorithms).
- Avoid per-frame Painter allocation by storing painter(s) inside screen state and resizing only on terminal resize.
- Keep existing transitions/controls.

## Implementation Notes

Approach A (preferred):
- Extract effect *core* so it can render both:
  - cell-space (BackdropFx)
  - painter-space (Canvas adapter)
- Demo uses painter-space to keep the wow factor.

Approach B (acceptable interim):
- Use Backdrop-only rendering for metaballs/plasma in the demo (lower-res but still good), while other effects keep using painter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No per-frame Painter allocations in steady state.
- [ ] Metaballs and Plasma demo visuals are driven by library code.
- [ ] No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:14Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Plan Update: Split Into Phases (Unblock Perf Work)

This issue was previously blocked on `bd-l8x9.3`/`.4` (extracted effects) and `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters).

That made the demo perf fix unnecessarily wait on library extraction.

New structure:
- Phase 1: stop per-frame allocations in `VisualEffectsScreen` (Painter reuse + resize-only growth)
- Phase 2: switch metaballs/plasma to the extracted `ftui-extras` BackdropFx implementation using **cell-space** rendering (lowest coupling)
- Phase 3 (optional): switch metaballs/plasma back to **hi-res** via canvas adapters (keeping the wow factor) once adapters exist

Each phase is tracked as its own subtask with the correct blocking dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","title":"Demo Perf: Reuse Painter Buffers (No Per-Frame Allocations)","description":"## Task
Refactor VisualEffectsScreen to reuse Painter/Canvas buffers across frames (grow-only), eliminating steady-state allocations.

## Scope
- Cache painter buffers in screen state.
- Resize-only growth; clear on size changes.
- Keep per-effect data isolated from shared buffers.

## Tests / Validation
- Allocation test: render 1000 frames and assert no allocations after warm-up.
- Unit test: resize triggers buffer growth and clears appropriately.
- Perf log: record per-frame time before/after and include in JSONL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero allocations after warm-up at stable terminal size.
- No visual regressions vs baseline snapshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:36.332215704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Notes

Target file:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Current anti-pattern:
- `Painter::for_area(...)` per frame (allocates `pixels` + `colors` vectors every frame).

Refactor sketch:
- Store one or more `Painter` instances inside `VisualEffectsScreen` state.
- Track the last required subpixel dimensions and `Mode`.
- On render:
  - compute required subpixel dims from `area` and `Mode`
  - if dims unchanged: `painter.clear()` and reuse
  - if dims grew: reallocate once, then reuse
  - if dims shrank: keep capacity; just treat as smaller logical size

Validation:
- Run `./scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (screen navigation exercises the visual effects screen).
- Add a focused no-alloc check (unit test or debug-only counter) that asserts no `Vec` growth occurs after the first render at a stable size.

Acceptance:
- [ ] steady-state frames in VisualEffectsScreen allocate 0 bytes
- [ ] resize triggers at most one reallocation per growth event
- [ ] no behavior regressions (transitions/controls still work)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","title":"Demo Integration Phase 2: Use Extracted Metaballs/Plasma Backdrops (Cell-Space)","description":"Switch VisualEffectsScreen metaballs/plasma to call the extracted ftui-extras BackdropFx implementations via the Backdrop widget (cell-space rendering). This removes duplicated algorithms and validates composability; hi-res canvas output can return later via adapters.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Demo Integration Phase 2: Use Extracted Metaballs/Plasma Backdrops (Cell-Space)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs
- If any palette/gradient math: specify the color space + constraints (e.g., OKLCH) and prove WCAG targets in tests
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:10:12.798180703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Notes

This phase intentionally prioritizes **code reuse + composability validation** over maximum visual fidelity.

Why:
- It proves the extracted `BackdropFx` + `Backdrop` widget can drive a real screen.
- It eliminates duplicated metaballs/plasma logic inside `visual_effects.rs`.
- It gives us a working baseline while `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters) remains optional.

Implementation outline:
- In `VisualEffectsScreen::view()`:
  - for `EffectType::Metaballs` and `EffectType::Plasma`, render a `Backdrop<MetaballsFx/PlasmaFx>` into the screen area.
  - render any existing help/FPS overlay text after the backdrop so it remains readable.
- Keep the rest of the effects unchanged (they can continue using the existing canvas painter).

Acceptance:
- [ ] metaballs/plasma visual output is driven by `ftui-extras` (no local algorithm copies)
- [ ] screen transitions and keyboard controls unchanged
- [ ] no per-frame allocations (relies on Phase 1)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","title":"Demo Integration Phase 3 (Optional): Hi-Res Metaballs/Plasma via Canvas Adapters","description":"Restore the showcase 'wow' for metaballs/plasma by using the extracted effects via canvas adapters (Braille/Block) instead of low-res cell backgrounds, while still sharing the same core algorithms.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Demo Integration Phase 3 (Optional): Hi-Res Metaballs/Plasma via Canvas Adapters
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs
- If any palette/gradient math: specify the color space + constraints (e.g., OKLCH) and prove WCAG targets in tests
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:11:04.546769166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Notes

This is the fidelity step: once `bd-l8x9.6` exists, the demo can regain sub-cell resolution effects while still using the library code.

Implementation outline:
- Replace the Phase 2 cell-space backdrop rendering with:
  - `MetaballsFx::render_to_painter(...)` (via adapter)
  - `PlasmaFx::render_to_painter(...)` (via adapter)
  - `Canvas::from_painter(&painter)` to render
- Keep Painter reuse from Phase 1.

Acceptance:
- [ ] metaballs/plasma match (or exceed) current wow factor
- [ ] no duplicated core algorithms inside demo
- [ ] no per-frame allocations","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:27Z"}]}
@@ -782,7 +782,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.8","title":"Testing: Backdrop Blending Correctness + Determinism","description":"## Goal
Add comprehensive test coverage for visual backdrops so we can refactor aggressively without regressions.

This bead covers:
- unit tests for correctness/determinism
- E2E coverage via existing bash/PTY harnesses (with detailed logging)

## Unit Tests (Fast + Deterministic)
- Background-only invariant: Backdrop must not mutate glyph content.
- Alpha blending correctness: expected results match `PackedRgba::over()` / `Buffer::set()` semantics.
- Determinism: fixed inputs -> stable output hash.
- Tiny-area safety: 0x0/1x1/1xN/Nx1 does not panic.

## E2E Coverage (Actionable Logs)
- `bd-l8x9.8.1`: include VisualEffects screen in `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` startup loop.
- `bd-l8x9.8.2`: add targeted runs for markdown-over-backdrop + metaballs/plasma paths.
- `bd-l8x9.8.3` (optional): Rust PTY test using `ftui-pty` to drive key inputs reliably.

## Logging Requirements
- Reuse the existing E2E script style: per-step log files + summary table.
- On failure: print reproduction command(s) and point to the log directory.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and CI-friendly (no timing dependence).
- Failures are debuggable without interactive terminals.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:24.649213451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:18.420710104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:24.649213451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:28.676715863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":187,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Tests To Add

1) Background-only invariant
- Start with a frame where cells have known glyph content.
- Apply Backdrop.
- Assert glyph content is unchanged (Backdrop must only touch bg).

2) Alpha blending correctness
- Use a tiny buffer (e.g., 2x2).
- Fill base bg with known color.
- Apply a deterministic effect output with known RGBA.
- Assert resulting bg matches `PackedRgba::over` expectations.

3) Determinism
- Given fixed effect params + time, render into out buffer twice and hash.

4) Tiny area safety
- 1x1, 1xN, Nx1, and very small rects must not panic.

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests are fast and deterministic (no timing dependence)
- [ ] tests live in the fx/backdrop module or adjacent tests
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.8.1","title":"E2E Phase 1: Cover VisualEffects Screen (Add Screen 12 To demo_showcase_e2e.sh)","description":"## Goal
Ensure `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` actually exercises the VisualEffects screen.

## Context
`ftui-demo-showcase` currently has **12** screens (`ScreenId::ALL` in `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`), but the E2E script only starts screens `1..11`.

That means VisualEffects regressions can slip through CI even though it is one of the highest-risk screens.

## Change
- Update the Step 7 loop in `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh`:
  - from `1..11` to `1..12`
- Update step labels/comments from "all 11" -> "all 12".

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E script starts screen 12 and reports failures with the correct screen number.
- Logs remain high-signal and point to the per-step log file on failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:38.079140462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:25.435098874Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","e2e","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:38.079140462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":211,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context / Bug

`ftui-demo-showcase` currently has **12** screens (see `ScreenId::ALL` in `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`), but `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` only starts screens `1..11`.

This means VisualEffects (VFX) regressions can slip through CI even though it is one of the heaviest/riskiest screens.

## Changes

- Update `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` Step 7 loop:
  - from: `for screen_num in 1 .. 11`
  - to: `for screen_num in 1 .. 12`
- Update step labels/comments from "all 11" -> "all 12".

## Optional Follow-Up (Nice-to-have)

Update `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` help text screen list to include screen 12 and correct numbering so humans and scripts agree.

## Acceptance

- [ ] E2E script starts VFX screen as part of navigation smoke checks
- [ ] logs clearly include the new screen number on failures","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:46Z"},{"id":216,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up for correcting CLI help text/screen numbering is tracked explicitly in .","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:05:51Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up for correcting CLI help text/screen numbering is tracked explicitly in bd-l8x9.5.4.","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:06:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.8.2","title":"E2E Phase 2: Backdrop Scenarios (Markdown-Over-FX + Metaballs/Plasma Paths)","description":"## Goal
Extend the demo showcase E2E suite with targeted runs that specifically exercise the new backdrop integration paths.

## Scenarios

1) Markdown-over-backdrop
- Start on the Markdown screen (`--screen=11` per `ScreenId::ALL`).
- Run long enough to animate a few frames (`FTUI_DEMO_EXIT_AFTER_MS=2500`).
- Validates:
  - Backdrop composition wrapper
  - scrim defaults
  - markdown renderer stays stable over animated backgrounds

2) VisualEffects metaballs/plasma (library path)
- Start on VisualEffects (`--screen=12`).
- Run long enough to render multiple frames.
- After bd-l8x9.5.2 lands, this ensures metaballs/plasma are driven by `ftui-extras` code.

## Logging Requirements
- Reuse existing `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` logging style.
- On failure: print the last chunk of the failing log file in the summary (so CI is debuggable without downloading artifacts).

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E reliably covers markdown-over-backdrop and VFX metaballs/plasma paths.
- Failures provide actionable logs and reproduction commands.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:55.686370014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:34.990825479Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","e2e","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:55.686370014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:55.686370014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:55.686370014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:00:55.686370014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":212,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Proposed Additions To scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh

Add one or two dedicated steps after the existing navigation smoke loop:

1) Markdown backdrop run (stability + animation)
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` starting on the Markdown screen (`--screen=11` as of `ScreenId::ALL`)
- `FTUI_DEMO_EXIT_AFTER_MS=2500` (ensures a few render ticks)
- If `bd-l8x9.7` adds an animated backdrop, this will exercise it.

2) VFX metaballs/plasma run (library path)
- Run starting on the VisualEffects screen (`--screen=12`)
- Let it run ~2500ms
- Optional: if we add an env var later like `FTUI_DEMO_VFX_EFFECT=plasma`, add a second run that starts directly in plasma.

Logging improvements:
- On failure, print the last ~200 lines of the log file to stderr in the summary (keeps CI debuggable without downloading artifacts).
- Persist a minimal environment snapshot (rustc/cargo versions + git rev + enabled features) for these new steps.

Acceptance:
- [ ] E2E covers the new markdown-over-backdrop composition path
- [ ] E2E covers the VFX metaballs/plasma path after refactor
- [ ] Logs remain high signal and actionable on failure","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:05Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","title":"E2E (Rust/PTy): Drive VFX Inputs (Cycle Effects/Palettes) Without Panics","description":"Add a Rust-based PTY integration test (using ftui-pty) that spawns ftui-demo-showcase on the VisualEffects screen, sends keypress bytes (space/left/right/p), and asserts the app stays alive + exits cleanly (no panic, no terminal corruption).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: E2E (Rust/PTy): Drive VFX Inputs (Cycle Effects/Palettes) Without Panics
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs
- If any palette/gradient math: specify the color space + constraints (e.g., OKLCH) and prove WCAG targets in tests
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:14.765903349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:11:04.074174089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:14.765903349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:14.765903349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why Rust-Based (Not Bash)

The demo reads real terminal events via crossterm. Sending keystrokes reliably from bash is fragile.

`ftui-pty` already exists to provide deterministic PTY automation; use it to:
- start the process with a known terminal size
- write input bytes to the PTY master (e.g., space / 'p' / arrow keys escape sequences)
- capture output for debugging if the test fails

## Test Outline

- Spawn: `cargo run -p ftui-demo-showcase -- --screen=12` (or call the built binary)
- Set env: `FTUI_DEMO_EXIT_AFTER_MS=0` and enforce a hard test timeout externally.
- Send inputs with sleeps between:
  - space (next effect)
  - 'p' (cycle palette on plasma)
  - left/right arrows (escape sequences)
- After N interactions, request quit ('q') and assert clean exit.

## Logging

On failure, dump:
- captured stderr
- last chunk of stdout escape stream (trimmed)
- which key was last sent

Acceptance:
- [ ] test is deterministic and runs in CI (no interactive requirements)
- [ ] proves VFX input handling survives rapid switches","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:26Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","title":"E2E (Rust/PTy): Drive VFX Inputs (Cycle Effects/Palettes) Without Panics","description":"## Goal
Add a Rust-based PTY integration test that drives the VisualEffects screen with real key input and asserts the app never panics.

## Why Rust/PTY (Not Bash)
The demo reads real terminal events via crossterm; sending complex input sequences from bash is fragile.

`ftui-pty` already exists to provide deterministic PTY automation.

## Test Outline
- Spawn `ftui-demo-showcase` on VisualEffects (`--screen=12`).
- Send key inputs with small delays:
  - space / left / right (cycle effects)
  - 'p' (cycle plasma palette when plasma is active)
- Quit cleanly ('q') and assert exit success.

## Logging Requirements
- On failure, print:
  - which key was last sent
  - captured stderr
  - a trimmed tail of captured output

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test runs deterministically in CI (no interactive requirements).
- Proves VFX input handling survives rapid switches without panics.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:14.765903349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:42.268917420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:14.765903349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:14.765903349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why Rust-Based (Not Bash)

The demo reads real terminal events via crossterm. Sending keystrokes reliably from bash is fragile.

`ftui-pty` already exists to provide deterministic PTY automation; use it to:
- start the process with a known terminal size
- write input bytes to the PTY master (e.g., space / 'p' / arrow keys escape sequences)
- capture output for debugging if the test fails

## Test Outline

- Spawn: `cargo run -p ftui-demo-showcase -- --screen=12` (or call the built binary)
- Set env: `FTUI_DEMO_EXIT_AFTER_MS=0` and enforce a hard test timeout externally.
- Send inputs with sleeps between:
  - space (next effect)
  - 'p' (cycle palette on plasma)
  - left/right arrows (escape sequences)
- After N interactions, request quit ('q') and assert clean exit.

## Logging

On failure, dump:
- captured stderr
- last chunk of stdout escape stream (trimmed)
- which key was last sent

Acceptance:
- [ ] test is deterministic and runs in CI (no interactive requirements)
- [ ] proves VFX input handling survives rapid switches","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.8.4","title":"E2E: Update widget_api_e2e.sh Feature Matrix (Include visual-fx / fx-gpu)","description":"Extend scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh feature-combination checks to cover the new ftui-extras FX feature flags (CPU + GPU-gated). Ensures CI catches feature-graph breakage early and logs the failing combination clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The ftui-extras feature matrix includes: `visual-fx`, `visual-fx,canvas`, `visual-fx,fx-gpu` (and `visual-fx,fx-gpu,canvas` if applicable).
- [ ] For each combination, the script prints the exact cargo command executed.
- [ ] On failure, logs include: feature set, exit code, and the last ~200 lines of cargo output.
- [ ] CI output is high-signal and points directly at the failing combination.

## Tests / Validation
- E2E script emits JSONL summary per feature combination (run_id, features, status, timings).
- Capture artifact path for failing combo logs.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:26.473326416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:39.709649083Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:26.473326416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:26.473326416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":220,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.8.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What To Add

In `scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh` (Step 4: feature combinations), extend the ftui-extras matrix to include:
- `visual-fx` (CPU baseline)
- `visual-fx,canvas` (ensure no feature conflicts)
- `visual-fx,fx-gpu` (GPU builds compile)

Optional but useful:
- `visual-fx,fx-gpu,canvas` (if adapters live behind canvas)

## Logging Requirements

- For each combination, print the exact cargo command executed.
- On failure, print:
  - the feature set
  - exit code
  - the last ~200 lines of cargo output (so CI logs are actionable).

## Acceptance

- [ ] CI can compile ftui-extras with the new FX feature flags
- [ ] Failures are high-signal and easy to diagnose","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.9","title":"Performance: FX Backdrop Hot Path Profiling + Benchmarks","description":"## Task
Profile FX backdrop hot paths and establish performance budgets.

## Scope
- Capture CPU flamegraphs for Metaballs/Plasma and Backdrop composition.
- Establish p50/p95 budgets at 80x24 and 120x40.

## Tests / Validation
- Microbench harness with JSONL logging (seed, size, quality, timings).
- Regression check with documented budget thresholds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hotspots identified with remediation notes.
- Budgets committed for each FX at default quality.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:44.686127862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:31.029525084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:44.686127862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:52.435790102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:52.605142924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What To Measure

- Backdrop application cost: applying a precomputed bg buffer to `frame.buffer`.
- Effect computation cost: MetaballsFx and PlasmaFx generating the bg buffer.

## Target Budgets (rough)

- 120x40 (4800 cells): backdrop+effect should be comfortably sub-frame (< ~2-4ms on dev machine).
- Large terminals should trigger Reduced/Minimal quality.

## Bench Strategy

- Add Criterion benches (or lightweight perf tests) that:
  - run fixed-size renders
  - report ns/cell
  - validate no allocations in steady state

## Acceptance

- [ ] We can quantify cost per effect + per resolution.
- [ ] Quality mapping avoids worst-case runaway costs.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:37:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mevj","title":"Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests","description":"# Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests

## Background
Part of Headless Rendering Mode (bd-3m7u). Tests ensure headless mode works for CI testing.

## Unit Tests
- headless_term_processes_ansi_correctly: CSI sequences update screen state
- cursor_movement_works: CSI H, CSI A/B/C/D move cursor correctly
- style_state_tracked: SGR sequences update style
- screen_text_returns_content: screen_text() matches expected output
- assert_matches_works: Assertion helper functions correctly

## Integration Tests
- snapshot_test_workflow: Full snapshot test with HeadlessTerm
- complex_layout_renders_correctly: Nested widgets render as expected
- style_codes_produce_correct_output: Colors and attributes work

## Property Tests
- any_ansi_sequence_processed_safely: Malformed sequences dont crash
- cursor_stays_in_bounds: Cursor never goes outside screen

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests verify real CI workflow
- [ ] Headless mode matches real terminal behavior
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:13.872368352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978575591Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978505329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mevj","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:20.202208063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
